entries.summary: Gunmen killed 26 tourists at Pahalgam in Indian-administered Kashmir, mostly targeting Hindu men.
India suspended Indus Waters Treaty participation and revoked all visas issued to Pakistani citizens.
Both countries shuttered airspace, expelled diplomats, and closed shared land borders.
Iran proposed mediating between New Delhi and Islamabad to prevent further escalation.
Troops exchanged small-arms fire across the Line of Control for second straight night.
Pakistan's foreign ministry threatened pulling out of the 1972 Simla Agreement.
India struck nine sites in Pakistan and Pakistan-administered Kashmir under Operation Sindoor.
Pakistan claimed it downed five Indian aircraft, including three French-made Rafale jets.
Pakistan said missile attacks killed at least 31 civilians, including two children.
Cross-border drone exchanges intensified between the nuclear-armed neighbours.
At least 50 deaths reported across both sides during three days of fighting.
Islamabad's Operation Bunyan Marsoos targeted six Indian military installations in retaliatory campaign.
Trump announced ceasefire on Truth Social, effective at 5pm Indian Standard Time.
Modi declared India only paused military action and warned against nuclear blackmail.
Pakistan promoted army chief Asim Munir to the rank of Field Marshal.
Indian defence chief Anil Chauhan admitted jet losses at Singapore's Shangri-La Dialogue.
Pakistan increased its defence budget by 20 percent following the May conflict.
Trump hosted Pakistan's army chief at the White House without civilian officials present.
Pakistan announced it would nominate Trump for the Nobel Peace Prize.
India stated the Indus Waters Treaty would remain suspended indefinitely.
Modi addressed parliament for 102 minutes, framing Operation Sindoor as ongoing.
Pakistan formally activated its Army Rocket Force Command, introducing new long-range missiles.
India defeated Pakistan in the Asia Cup cricket final amid lingering tensions.
Washington notified Congress of a proposed $686m upgrade for Pakistan's F-16 fleet.
Militants killed 26 civilians, mostly tourists, in Pahalgam, Indian-administered Kashmir.
Indian security teams investigated the killings at the Pahalgam attack site.
India and Pakistan cancelled each other's visas amid rapid diplomatic deterioration.
India's navy carried out long-range precision missile test firings off coast.
Pakistan downed an Indian espionage drone over disputed Kashmir, officials told CNN.
DGMOs of India and Pakistan held first hotline call since the Pahalgam attack.
Pakistan's information minister claimed credible intelligence about imminent Indian military action.
Rubio called Indian and Pakistani leaders, urging both sides to de-escalate.
India barred Pakistani commercial flights from its airspace, matching Islamabad's earlier prohibition.
Vance told Fox News he hoped India's response would not spark wider conflict.
India launched Operation Sindoor with missile strikes on nine targets across Pakistan.
Pakistan PM Sharif denounced India's overnight strikes as cowardly in parliament.
Trump announced US-mediated ceasefire halting four days of cross-border fighting.
Both governments accused each other of violating the ceasefire within hours.
Indian air force highlighted operation's precision; defence minister praised counterterrorism message.
Both governments publicly claimed victory while playing down their respective losses.
